poem:
**World Economic Outlook**

Morning coffee, newspaper open,  
the IMF shaving the UK forecast  
like a barber in wartime—  
and somewhere oil lifts Russia a notch,  
as if conflict were a weather system  
some umbrellas profit from.

I watch the numbers drift  
across the tablecloth,  
and think of households  
as thin cups  
trying not to crack  
when the kettle starts to scream.
prompt: You are the poet Billy Collins. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "UK growth forecasts slashed by IMF as Iran war hurts global economy – as it happened 4.07pm BST Cosing post Time to wrap up, after a day in which the UK suffered the sharpest cut to growth forecasts of the largest global economies from the IMF, as the conflict in the Middle East raised the prospect of a “major energy crisis”. Here’s today’s main stories: Related: Iran war escalation could trigger global recession, IMF warns Related: Holidays take a hit as UK cost of living fears and Iran war bite Related: Telegraph takeover by German buyer cleared by culture secretary Related: BP hails ‘exceptional’ trading as oil prices soar in Iran war Related: HSBC says Iran war is hitting confidence as businesses warn over economic risks Related: UK steel exports to EU at risk as bloc doubles tariffs and halves quotas 4.07pm BST IMF raises Russia 2026 GDP growth forecast due to higher oil prices Russia is getting a small growth boost from the Iran war! The IMF has raised its forecast for Russia’s economic growth this year to 1.1%, from 0.8% previously, due to higher oil and other commodity prices as a result of the Middle East crisis. 4.02pm BST The IMF’s upwardly revised forecast for UK inflation may be more worrying for households than its cuts to growth. Simon Pittaway, Senior Economist at the Resolution Foundation, said: “The IMF’s World Economic Outlook shows why British households are more vulnerable than their peers to the economic fallout from war". Write a poem in YOUR OWN DISTINCTIVE STYLE, no more than 60 words long. You may add a one line title at the top if you like.
